headline: The exit decision is governed by an irreversible financial cliff (census), yet the page that should state the date canonicalises to "Page not found", every decision page is cloned across two parallel hub trees, and the deciding knowledge is offloaded to a third FAQ host while the action itself hides behind login.
what_exists: The hub authors ~7 unique decision pages for this stage — leave-of-absence, course-withdrawal, subject-withdrawal, re-enrol-in-your-course, enrolment-cancellation-and-reinstatement, withdrawing-from-overseas-study, and census-dates — and the writing is genuinely good. The leave-of-absence page (students.unimelb.edu.au/course-admin/leave-of-absence; 209,333 bytes, 47 links) explicitly teaches the census mechanic: "You do not require a Leave of Absence after the census date has passed... it is counted as part of your study load... this may have significant academic and financial implications... you may be required to attend an At Risk or Course Academic Progress Committee (CAPC) meeting." It also states the rule that LoA is barred in the first six months (defer the offer before census instead) and carries a layered "Additional information for student visa holders" section. The enrolment-cancellation page names the real money — a $400 reinstatement fee under CFE/CIN sanctions, with a separate Fee Waiver Request Form. None of these pages are orphaned; they are well-linked. So the problem is NOT missing content — it is that the same content is multiplied across trees, while the decisive facts (dates, refund amounts, deadlines) and the transactional action both live on OTHER hosts.
friction: HIGH-CONSEQUENCE — the census cliff is irreversible and expensive: withdraw or take leave AFTER census = pay the full subject's fees AND record an academic fail AND trigger an At-Risk/CAPC referral. The student must get the timing exactly right precisely when, as an at-risk student, they are least able to.

The page a student MUST consult before acting — census-dates — lists no actual dates. The live /course-admin/census-dates page punts the date lookup to my.unimelb (behind login) and the Handbook, and offloads everything else to 10 separate ask.unimelb FAQ articles (Fees due dates a_id/925, Student Invoice a_id/6005, HECS-HELP a_id/5179...).

FIND→ACT BREAK: the leave action is a login-walled form at my.unimelb.edu.au/sone/LOA/ ('Form Responses'); withdrawal sends the student to a bare my.unimelb.edu.au/. The public page describes the decision but cannot transact it — the action is behind the wall in my.unimelb / *.app form microsites.

The deciding KNOWLEDGE is itself off-page. Withdrawal deadlines (ask.unimelb a_id/5046), whether fees are refunded (a_id/946), the 'interactive subject withdrawal options tool' (a_id/6253) and fee remission in special circumstances (a_id/980) all live on ask.unimelb — a separate FAQ host — not on the withdrawal page. To make one decision the student crosses students.unimelb → ask.unimelb → my.unimelb.

A $400 reinstatement fee and CFE/CIN account sanctions can be incurred passively by simply failing to re-enrol on time — a lapse-by-inaction trap for a disengaging at-risk student — with relief gated behind yet another form (Fee Waiver Request Form).

International students face the consequence stacked on the same page: a leave/load change that is academically routine can breach a visa condition, and the leave-of-absence page layers 'Additional information for student visa holders' on top of the general rule rather than giving visa-holders a clean path.

ask.unimelb links are emitted with mixed http:// and https:// schemes (e.g. http://ask.unimelb.edu.au/app/answers/detail/a_id/5046), an avoidable insecure-redirect/trust wrinkle on the exact links carrying the highest-stakes guidance.
fragmentation: PARALLEL HUB TREES: every concept in this stage is served from BOTH /course-admin/ and /your-course/manage-your-course/ — 15 live URLs that are really ~7 unique pages doubled. The clones are near-byte-identical: leave-of-absence 209,333 bytes / 47 links on both trees; course-withdrawal 184,906 / 42 on both; subject-withdrawal 188,225 / 45 on both; re-enrol 227,945 / 59 on both. A direct diff of the two leave-of-absence pages shows only 8 lines differ — all third-party tracking (an Optimizely iframe), not content.

A THIRD legacy tree, /admin/, is half-dead: /admin/census-dates and /admin/re-enrol both return title 'Page not found' with canonical = /page-not-found, while sibling /admin/ pages (fees/pay-my-fees, special) still resolve. Students arriving on old bookmarked /admin/ links hit a dead end for the two most time-critical actions.

THE CENSUS CANONICAL FAULT, precisely: /admin/census-dates has canonical + og:url = students.unimelb.edu.au/page-not-found (the authoritative date page tells search engines it does not exist); /course-admin/census-dates is the true self-canonical page; /your-course/.../subject-enrolment/census-dates correctly canonicalises back to /course-admin. So one of three census URLs actively de-indexes itself.

CROSS-HOST RELIANCE: students.unimelb emits 444 contextual links to ask.unimelb (FAQ host) versus only 187 to my.unimelb (action portal). For this stage the hub leans more on a separate knowledge base than on the place where the student actually acts — knowledge and action are split across two off-hub hosts.

FACULTY RE-INVENTION: Education (education.unimelb.edu.au/study/graduate-research/current-students/reviewscandidature-variations/leave-of-absence) and Law (law.unimelb.edu.au/students/masters/studies/leave-of-absence) re-host their own leave-of-absence pages for HDR/masters cohorts, adding faculty-specific variants on top of the two hub clones.

VISA-SIDE DRAFT LEAK: the enrolment-change-affecting-visa guidance also exists on the publicly-indexed /sandbox/2026-uplifts draft tree (e.g. /sandbox/2026-uplifts/student-visas/meet-your-student-visa-and-enrolment-requirements/manage-enrolment-changes-that-affect-your-visa), so the international slice of this decision has a fourth live location.
the_seam: The census date is the seam — and it is a hard, dated, irreversible boundary that sits BETWEEN the three things a student needs and that the estate scatters across three hosts: (1) the rule/consequence (on students.unimelb, but cloned across /course-admin + /your-course and partly dead on /admin), (2) the actual date and the deadline/refund facts (split between my.unimelb behind login, the Handbook, and ~10 ask.unimelb FAQs), and (3) the action — apply for leave / withdraw (my.unimelb.edu.au/sone/LOA/, behind the wall). No single page lets a student see the date, understand 'before vs after census', and act. The decision that turns on one date forces a three-host scavenger hunt (students.unimelb → ask.unimelb → my.unimelb) at the exact moment the at-risk student is least equipped to run it.
recommendation: Build one canonical "Take a break or leave" decision page per concept (leave, subject-withdrawal, course-withdrawal, re-enrol) that puts the student's own census date, the before/after-census fee + academic consequence, and the action button on a single page — pulling the date live from my.unimelb and inlining the deciding facts (deadlines, refund, fee-remission) instead of bouncing to ask.unimelb. Structurally: (1) FIX the census canonical now — 301 /admin/census-dates and /admin/re-enrol off /page-not-found to the live page; collapse /course-admin and /your-course to ONE tree with the other 301'd (kills the ~7 byte-identical clone pairs); delete the /sandbox/2026-uplifts visa drafts from the public index. (2) Surface the action: link the leave/withdraw pages directly to the my.unimelb LOA/withdrawal form and label it as an action, not 'Form Responses'. (3) Normalise every ask.unimelb link to https and, where a fact is decision-critical (deadline, refund, $400 reinstatement fee), inline it on the page rather than linking out. (4) Add a passive-lapse guardrail to the re-enrol page that foregrounds the $400 CFE/CIN consequence and waiver path for disengaging students. Treat first within at-risk: the cost of a timing error here is a full subject's fees, an academic fail, and — for visa holders — a compliance breach.

headline: The HDR front-of-funnel is forked across three competing landing hubs (study.unimelb, research.unimelb, and faculty CMSes), the supervisor-match tool is a faceted search deep-linked onto the off-estate findanexpert host, and the apply action lives on faculty/gradresearch. The most consequential decision in a research degree (who will supervise me, and will I get in) is stitched together from ~9 prospective pages plus a hop into another domain, with ZERO presence on the current-student hub.
what_exists: Prospective HDR discovery is THIN and TRIPLICATED across hosts. (1) study.unimelb carries the templated funnel: 1 landing (/study-with-us/graduate-research) + 8 pages under /how-to-apply/graduate-research (domestic and international, each with landing + entry-requirements + fees-and-payments, plus /joint-phds) = ~9 core prospective pages, matching the persona's "~6 prospective research-degree pages" (lifecycle-traces.json item 2, "HDR / research candidate", severity high). The study landing's nav exposes supervisor-matching as two deep-links into ANOTHER host, extracted verbatim from the page.html: "Find a supervisor" -> findanexpert.unimelb.edu.au/searchresults?category=profile with a supervision_availability facet, and "Find a project" -> findanexpert.unimelb.edu.au/searchresults?category=opportunity. (2) A SECOND prospective landing sits on the research office: research.unimelb.edu.au/graduate-research ("Research opportunities in Melbourne: PhD and graduate degree"), which links its OWN supervisor finder (research.unimelb/home/find-an-expert) plus a joint-PhD cluster (Industry PhD Programs; KU Leuven, RWTH Aachen, Jerusalem-Melbourne joint-PhD groups); research.unimelb was crawled at 662 pages. (3) A THIRD layer is faculty-hosted: ~41 faculty prospective research-degree pages (education /study/graduate-research 62, fbe /study/phd 17, arts /students/graduate-research, msd /research/graduate-research) per prospective-experience-profiles.json. The application is then transacted on faculty CMSes / gradresearch.unimelb (off the crawled estate). The current-student hub students.unimelb holds NONE of this discover/supervisor/apply layer: all 15 of its "graduate-research" URLs are candidature-stage or unrelated (10x /academic-skills/graduate-research-services writing support; /support-and-wellbeing/.../under-18-international-student-supervision-program; /course-admin/study-overseas USA-travel update) and it makes only 60 contextual links out to gradresearch (cross-site-flow.csv).
friction: FIND -> ACT BREAK on the supervisor decision: the discovery page lives on study.unimelb but the supervisor-matching ACTION is a deep-link onto a different host (findanexpert.unimelb.edu.au/searchresults?category=profile + supervision_availability facet). The student leaves the prospective site, lands on a research-profile search engine branded 'Find an Expert' (not 'find a supervisor'), and must self-serve a faceted query with no guidance on how to read availability, contact etiquette, or what a good match looks like. This is the highest-consequence moment of the journey and it is a raw URL handoff.

THREE COMPETING FRONT DOORS: a prospective HDR can legitimately land on study.unimelb/study-with-us/graduate-research, research.unimelb/graduate-research, OR a faculty /study/graduate-research page — each a different visual system, each with its OWN 'find an expert' entry point (study -> findanexpert searchresults; research -> research.unimelb/home/find-an-expert). There is no single canonical 'start your PhD here', so discovery depends on which door Google surfaces.

THE APPLY ACTION IS OFF-ESTATE AND UNSEEN: the actual application is submitted via faculty CMSes / gradresearch.unimelb, which sits OUTSIDE the crawled student estate (lifecycle-traces.json: 'the canonical owner gradresearch.unimelb sits OUTSIDE the crawled student estate — the journey's spine is off to the side'). The public pages describe applying; the transaction is one more hop away on a host the prospective student has not yet seen.

THE RESEARCH PARADOX (high-consequence mismatch): the faculties with the LARGEST research estates carry the LEAST HDR-student journey support — MDHS has 63 research pages but ~1 HDR-student support page (lifecycle-traces.json). Strongest research identity, weakest research-student funnel, so a student drawn by research strength finds the thinnest path to join.

BROKEN SUPERVISOR LINKS: faculty pages contain malformed findanexpert references that 404 — e.g. biomedicalsciences.unimelb.edu.au/.../www.findanexpert.unimelb.edu.au/display/person15723 and medicine.unimelb.edu.au/.../www.findanexpert.unimelb.edu.au/display/person147194 (dead-pages.csv) — plus arts.unimelb.edu.au/scholarships/graduate-research returns 404. When a student does try to reach a named potential supervisor's profile from a faculty page, the link can be dead.

REVERSE-FUNNELLED FEES: HDR fee/cost-of-candidature content is on the PROSPECTIVE site (study.unimelb/how-to-apply/graduate-research/domestic|international/fees-and-payments), not on the hub — consistent with the estate's wider reverse-funnel where decision detail lives on study.unimelb. A student weighing affordability of 3-4 years of candidature must stay on the marketing site to find it.
fragmentation: THREE prospective landing hubs for one decision: study.unimelb/study-with-us/graduate-research (the templated funnel, ~9 pages) vs research.unimelb/graduate-research ('Research opportunities in Melbourne', part of a 662-page research-office crawl) vs ~41 faculty /study/graduate-research | /study/phd | /students/graduate-research pages — three editorial systems, three owners, no single canonical entry.

TWO supervisor-finder entry points, both off the current-student estate: study.unimelb deep-links findanexpert.unimelb.edu.au/searchresults (profile + supervision_availability facet, and an opportunity/'find a project' facet), while research.unimelb routes to research.unimelb/home/find-an-expert — the same underlying 'Find an Expert' tool reached via two different wrappers under two different brands.

BYTE-IDENTICAL faculty clones of the downstream candidature lifecycle (the apply/onboard layer the prospect is about to enter): education /study/graduate-research/current-students/* and mdhs /research/research-training/current-students/* are Jaccard 1.0 across the entire reviewscandidature-variations set (pre-confirmation, confirmation, progress-review, at-risk, unsatisfactory-progress, making-changes), thesisexamination/* (writing-my-thesis, thesis-with-publication, submitting-my-thesis, my-thesis-in-the-library) and student-support-services/* (counselling, library, housing-support, safer-community-program) — same bytes, different URL trees, per near-duplicates.csv. ~8 faculty CMSes each re-host their own copy.

Off-estate spine: gradresearch.unimelb (the 'Graduate Research Hub', hub anchor 'Graduate Research Hub' x89) and findanexpert.unimelb are both OUTSIDE the crawled student estate; the journey's two load-bearing destinations (where you apply, where you find a supervisor) are not part of the current-student web the student will live in.

The current-student hub is absent from discovery: students.unimelb contributes nothing to discover/supervisor/apply (its 15 'graduate-research' URLs are candidature-stage academic-skills writing services + an unrelated under-18 supervision program), and links to gradresearch only 60 times — so the hub neither owns nor meaningfully signposts the front of the HDR funnel.
the_seam: The supervisor-matching handoff: study.unimelb's prospective HDR landing page describes finding a supervisor, but the ACTION is a raw deep-link onto a different host, findanexpert.unimelb.edu.au/searchresults?category=profile with a supervision_availability facet (and a parallel category=opportunity 'find a project' facet). This is the estate's signature find->act break applied to the single highest-stakes decision in the journey: the page that should help you choose a supervisor instead hands you a URL into an unguided faceted research-profile search engine branded 'Find an Expert', on a domain you have not seen, with no scaffolding on availability, contact etiquette, or what makes a viable match. The same seam is duplicated by research.unimelb pointing at its own /home/find-an-expert wrapper, so the one moment that determines whether a PhD even starts has two off-hub entry points and zero canonical owner.
recommendation: Make ONE canonical 'Start a research degree' front door (gradresearch.unimelb as the system of record, surfaced from both study.unimelb and the current-student hub) that owns discover -> find a supervisor -> apply as a single guided flow, and 301 the competing landings (research.unimelb/graduate-research, the ~41 faculty /study/graduate-research and /study/phd pages, education vs mdhs byte-identical clones) into it. Critically, convert the supervisor step from a raw findanexpert deep-link into a wrapped, guided match experience embedded in the funnel: pre-filter to supervision_availability, explain how to read availability and make first contact, and unify the two 'Find an Expert' wrappers (study's searchresults link and research's /home/find-an-expert) behind one branded 'Find a supervisor' tool. Fix the malformed/404 findanexpert profile links on faculty pages (e.g. biomedicalsciences and medicine /.../www.findanexpert.unimelb.edu.au/display/person... and arts /scholarships/graduate-research), and pull the HDR cost-of-candidature content forward so affordability isn't buried on the marketing site. This closes the find->act break on the highest-consequence moment and collapses three competing funnels into one.

headline: The candidature lifecycle's system of record sits entirely OFF the crawled student estate (gradresearch.unimelb — 0 hub pages), the milestone actions are behind login on tes.app, and two faculties re-host the spine byte-identical: 18 pages at Jaccard=1.000, 26 "Graduate Research Hub"-breadcrumb pages on each domain.
what_exists: The canonical candidature spine — confirmation, pre-confirmation, progress-review, unsatisfactory-progress/at-risk, making-changes, leave-of-absence, late/lapsed candidature, Study Away, supervision, thesis prep — lives on gradresearch.unimelb.edu.au ("Graduate Research Hub"), which is OFF the crawled student estate (no crawl dir exists; 0 candidature pages on students.unimelb). It is the most-referenced research destination in anchor-text.csv: "Graduate Research Hub" 89x, plus task anchors "Preparation of Graduate Research Theses" 40x, "Working with my supervisors" 39x, "Study Away"/"Apply for Study Away" 39x each, "Add or drop coursework subjects" 38x, "Mandatory training for graduate researchers" 38x. The submission milestone ACTION is the Thesis Examination System, tes.app.unimelb.edu.au — anchored "register your intention to submit" 6x and "Thesis Examination System (TES)" 4x — a login-walled *.app microsite (thesislibrary.app sits alongside for the deposited thesis). On the student hub itself, HDR content is almost entirely Academic Skills writing support (/academic-skills/graduate-research-services: writing-thesis-sections-part-1/2, incorporating-publications-in-your-thesis, writing-a-paper-for-publication) plus orientation (/new-students/get-started/research-domestic) and course-admin/study-overseas/programs-for-researchers — there is NOT a single candidature / milestone / confirmation / progress-review page on students.unimelb. The lifecycle's own pages do not live where the candidate logs in.
friction: HIGH-CONSEQUENCE — Confirmation & progress reviews are the milestones that can terminate candidature (the chain runs confirmation -> annual progress-review -> unsatisfactory-progress/at-risk -> show-cause -> termination). The authoritative guidance for all of these is off-estate on gradresearch (/reviewscandidature-variations/confirmation, /progress-review, /unsatisfactory-progress, /at-risk); a candidate who lives in my.unimelb + Canvas + the student hub has no path to them from where they actually are.

HIGH-CONSEQUENCE — The find->act break at the submission milestone: every candidature page only DESCRIBES the step; the action is on a separate login-walled microsite. 'Register your intention to submit' resolves to tes.app.unimelb.edu.au (TES), and the deposited thesis lands in thesislibrary.app — neither is on the public hub or the gradresearch describe-pages a candidate browses first.

Which copy am I reading? The same nominal candidature page exists on at least THREE hosts (gradresearch + education + mdhs). A candidate arriving from their faculty (education 1,972 links, mdhs 1,882) reads a faculty-hosted copy under a faculty breadcrumb that is actually the central GR-Hub content — with no signal it is the same policy maintained centrally, and a measurable risk it has drifted (see fragmentation).

Wrong front door — the student hub's HDR footprint is writing-skills, not candidature management, so a candidate searching the place they log in for 'confirmation' or 'progress review' finds Academic Skills resources, not the milestone process. The milestone spine is reachable only by leaving the estate.

The faculties with the most research barely connect to the spine: cross-site-flow.csv shows MSD 2, biomedical-sciences 3, finearts-music 7, medicine 16, science 18, engineering 19, FBE 20 contextual links to gradresearch — so a candidate in a research-intensive faculty other than Education/MDHS gets almost no in-context route to where the candidature lifecycle lives.
fragmentation: The spine is rehosted byte-identical on two faculty CMSes. education.unimelb /study/graduate-research/current-students/reviewscandidature-variations/<leaf> and mdhs.unimelb /research/research-training/current-students/reviewscandidature-variations/<leaf> carry the SAME leaf set (confirmation, pre-confirmation, progress-review, unsatisfactory-progress, at-risk, making-changes; education also leave-of-absence, late/lapsed-candidature, study-away, study-abroad-and-exchange). Verified directly: /confirmation is 20,785 chars on BOTH with 10-gram Jaccard = 1.000 (3,074/3,074 shared shingles); /progress-review is 21,416 chars on BOTH, IDENTICAL — and both render the breadcrumb 'Graduate Research Hub', i.e. they are literally gradresearch's pages served under faculty domains.

Scale: 26 pages on education AND 26 on mdhs carry the 'Graduate Research Hub' breadcrumb — the whole 'being a candidate' spine is mirrored, not just one page. The improvements-register's near_dup.py run fixes the precise count: of 33 shared path-tails, exactly 18 are Jaccard=1.000 byte-identical (confirmation 3,061w, thesis-with-publication 4,202w, unsatisfactory-progress 3,154w, writing-my-thesis 2,867w, my-thesis-in-the-library 3,573w + 13 more), maintained in triplicate (gradresearch + 2 faculties).

Silent drift is already happening — the cost case is live: of the same 33 path-tails, ~7 have DRIFTED to different content across the two faculties (financial-aid Jaccard 0.143, academic-skills 0.164, overseas-student-health-cover 0.116, renewing-your-student-visa 0.200) — the same nominal candidature-adjacent page now telling two faculties' HDR candidates different things. Every edit to identical policy must be made 2-3x; the drifted pages prove the copies diverge once forked.

The action layer is itself fragmented into single-purpose login-walled microsites: tes.app (intention-to-submit / examination), thesislibrary.app (deposited thesis), plus the shared HDR-relevant enrolmentvariations.app and specialconsideration.app — none indexed on the public hub, discoverable only via descriptive links from the off-estate spine or the faculty clones.

Adjacent HDR support is split off again: thesis writing-skills sit on students.unimelb/academic-skills/graduate-research-services while the candidature process sits on gradresearch — the same candidate's 'write my thesis' help and 'submit my thesis milestone' process are on different hosts under different owners.
the_seam: The seam is the boundary between the candidature SYSTEM OF RECORD (gradresearch.unimelb — off the crawled student estate) and the two surfaces the candidate actually inhabits: (a) the authenticated core they log into (my.unimelb / Canvas / tes.app, where the milestone actions live behind the wall) and (b) the students.unimelb hub + their own faculty site, where they look first. gradresearch owns the describe-content but neither transacts the milestone (that is tes.app, login-walled) nor appears on the hub; meanwhile education and mdhs paper over the gap by re-hosting the spine byte-identical (3,854 combined contextual links into gradresearch), so the SAME page exists in up to three places while the canonical owner is reachable from the hub on only ~60 links. The break is: the lifecycle's pages, the lifecycle's actions, and the place the candidate logs in are three different estates with no single authoritative thread between them.
recommendation: Make gradresearch.unimelb the single system of record for the candidature spine and bind it to the two surfaces candidates use. (1) Immediately 301 the 18 Jaccard=1.000 pages on BOTH education and mdhs (worklist = near-duplicates.csv cluster 4.1, the jaccard=1.0 rows) to their gradresearch canonical; replace with a thin faculty stub only where genuinely faculty-specific. (2) Reconcile the ~7 drifted pairs (financial-aid, academic-skills, OSHC, visa) to one gradresearch version BEFORE redirecting — use the drift as the stakeholder cost case. (3) Wire the milestone describe-pages directly to the action: put the tes.app 'register your intention to submit' / TES link and the enrolment-variation + special-consideration actions in-page on confirmation, progress-review and submission pages, closing the find->act break. (4) Give the candidature lifecycle a presence ON students.unimelb: a 'Current graduate researchers -> Graduate Research Hub' contextual block (supervision, milestones, thesis prep, Study Away) on the HDR/research landing pages of the under-connecting research faculties (MSD 2, biomed 3, ffam 7, medicine 16, science 18, eng 19, FBE 20 links today) so a candidate reaches the spine from where they log in rather than only by leaving the estate.
